name: LLaMA-Factory
tagline: Fine-tune 100+ open LLMs and multimodal models with one CLI or a web UI
metaDescription: LLaMA-Factory is an open-source training suite for pre-training, supervised fine-tuning, and RLHF across 100+ LLMs, with a zero-code CLI and web UI.
overview: LLaMA-Factory is an open-source training suite that lets you pre-train, supervised fine-tune, and run RLHF on large language models from one place. It supports 100+ text and multimodal model families, including LLaMA, Qwen3, Mistral, Mixtral-MoE, DeepSeek, Gemma, GLM, Phi, and LLaVA, and you drive everything through a command-line tool or a Gradio web UI called LLaMA Board.

It is aimed at ML engineers and researchers who want to adapt an existing open model to their own data without writing a custom training loop. You describe a training run in a YAML config and pass it to the CLI, or fill in the fields in the web UI, so most common fine-tuning recipes work with little or no code.

As a fine-tuning framework, it bundles the methods you would otherwise wire together yourself: full and freeze tuning, LoRA and 2-8 bit QLoRA, reward modeling, PPO, DPO, KTO, and ORPO, plus speed-ups like FlashAttention-2, Unsloth, and Liger Kernel. After training it can serve the model through an OpenAI-style API backed by a vLLM or SGLang worker.
features: Supports 100+ models, including LLaMA, Qwen3, Qwen3-VL, Mistral, Mixtral-MoE, DeepSeek, Gemma, GLM, Phi, and LLaVA
Covers (continuous) pre-training, (multimodal) supervised fine-tuning, reward modeling, PPO, DPO, KTO, and ORPO
Scales from 16-bit full tuning and freeze-tuning to LoRA and 2/3/4/5/6/8-bit QLoRA via AQLM, AWQ, GPTQ, and others
Includes training speed-ups such as FlashAttention-2, Unsloth, Liger Kernel, RoPE scaling, and NEFTune
Zero-code workflows via the llamafactory-cli command line and the LLaMA Board web UI
Serves trained models through an OpenAI-style API with a vLLM or SGLang worker, plus experiment tracking via TensorBoard, W&B, MLflow, and SwanLab

gettingStarted.intro: Install LLaMA-Factory from source, then run a LoRA fine-tuning, chat, and export cycle using the bundled example configs.

steps.heading: Install from source
steps.body: Clone the repository and install it in editable mode along with the metrics requirements.
steps.code:
git clone --depth 1 https://github.com/hiyouga/LlamaFactory.git
cd LlamaFactory
pip install -e .
pip install -r requirements/metrics.txt

steps.heading: Run LoRA fine-tuning
steps.body: Train a LoRA adapter using a provided example YAML config. Each config sets the model, dataset, and training arguments.

steps.heading: Chat with the fine-tuned model and export it
steps.body: Load the adapter to chat in the terminal, then merge it into a standalone model.
steps.code:
llamafactory-cli chat examples/inference/qwen3_lora_sft.yaml
llamafactory-cli export examples/merge_lora/qwen3_lora_sft.yaml

steps.heading: Use the web UI instead
steps.body: Prefer a no-code workflow? Launch LLaMA Board to configure and run training in the browser.

useCases: Adapt an open base model like Qwen3 or LLaMA to a domain dataset with LoRA or QLoRA on limited GPU memory
Align a model to human preferences using DPO, PPO, KTO, or ORPO without building a custom RLHF pipeline
Fine-tune multimodal models for image understanding, visual grounding, or video and audio tasks
Let non-experts run and compare training jobs through the LLaMA Board web UI, then serve results via an OpenAI-style API
